{{Infobox concept
| name = gate (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[gate (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy) Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A Gate serves as a portal or opening that connects the current world to various different Territories. These portals are utilized by individuals known as [[Travelers]], who possess the ability to conjure and manipulate them. The appearance and behavior of a Gate vary significantly depending on its origin; for example, [[Valinhall]] [[Gates]] typically manifest as white-edged swirling doorways that appear to be sliced into existence, while [[Endross Gates]] resemble miniature, flashing thunderstorms. Some [[Gates]], such as those from [[Tartarus]] or [[Naraka]], may emit distinct colors like red and carry specific environmental effects like wind or foul scents. The mechanics of a Gate are often tied to the specific [[Traveler]] and their [[Territory]]. [[Endross Gates]] are unique because they increase in size and power the longer they remain active, which can lead to a dangerous loss of control if the [[Traveler]] cannot restrain the unleashed energy. In contrast, certain [[Gates]], like those to [[Valinhall]], may require a specific [[Dragon's Fang]] to be held open. Additionally, some [[Gates]] are restricted by location, such as those to the [[Crimson Vault]], which can only be opened within the primary world rather than from within a [[Territory]]. While [[Travelers]] can close their [[Gates]], these openings can be forcibly reopened shortly after closing by using a device known as a [[gatecrawler]]. This tool allows others to follow a [[Traveler]] into a foreign [[Territory]] by tearing the recently sealed portal back open. Because [[Gates]] act as thresholds, they can be used to transport people, objects, or summoned creatures between worlds.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A Gate serves as a portal or defensive structure that creates an opening between the world and a [[Territory]]. While [[Leah]] used one to transport people, [[Travelers]] possess the ability to dissipate these openings at will. During a confrontation, [[Simon]] managed to tear open a Gate by reaching through his [[sword]] toward [[Valinhall]], though the aperture steadily shrunk as it began to seal itself. Elsewhere, a Gate leading to a red cavern appeared before [[Malachi]]. Because of the dangers involved, [[Travelers]] will attack [[Simon]] if they witness him attempting to open a Gate for [[Traveling]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A [[Traveler]] can dissipate their Gate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A Gate can be used to transport people and was opened by [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A Gate is an opening between the world and a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6">''House of Blades'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] can tear open a Gate by reaching through his [[sword]] to [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15">''House of Blades'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[The Gate]] shrunk steadily as it sealed itself.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A Gate to a red cavern appeared in front of [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A Gate is a defensive structure or portal.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|A Gate can be used for [[Traveling]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The [[Travelers]] will attack [[Simon]] if they see him try to open a Gate.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c22">''House of Blades'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate serves as a means of travel between different Territories, capable of opening onto a clearing or near a [[Hanging Tree]]. While these portals can be cut open and require significant strength to operate, they can also be closed using a [[frozen horn]]. One such Gate appeared suspended in midair with crackling white edges, revealing a view of a deep green forest. Other [[Gates]] have provided access to specific locations, such as a room within the royal palace in [[Cana]] or a way for [[Leah]] to return home.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[The Gate]] can open onto a clearing or near a [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate can be used to travel between Territories.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[frozen horn]] can be used to close [[Gates]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] uses a Gate to return home.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate appeared in midair, crackling white at the edges and showing a deep green forest.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate can be cut open.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] is unable to stop long enough to open a Gate.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate is something that requires strength to open.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate leads to a room in the royal palace in [[Cana]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The Gate]] manifests as a swirling object that shines with the brilliance of pure sunlight and possesses the ability to follow [[Alin]]. While [[Leah]] has demonstrated the power to shove individuals through a Gate, it is established that a [[Helgard Incarnation]] lacks the capability to open one to another [[Territory]]. During an encounter with a giant, an angry red Gate appeared behind the creature, though the Gate was later seen as clean and white. Ultimately, it remains possible that [[Indirial]] vanished into a Gate.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] can shove people through a Gate.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Helgard Incarnation]] cannot open a Gate to another [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The Gate]] is a swirling object that shines like pure sunlight and can follow [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An angry red Gate is behind the giant.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] may have vanished into a Gate.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The Gate]] was clean and white.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate concepts]]
